Skip to main content ☮️ Save the World Education Access

244 million children are out of school globally. The quality gap between rich and poor nations — and within nations — is enormous.

Education is the single most powerful lever for long-term development, yet it remains chronically underfunded in the places that need it most.
244 million

children are out of school globally
What Actually Works Teacher Training & Retention

The quality of education is directly tied to the quality of teaching. Investing in training, professional development, and competitive compensation for teachers is the highest-leverage intervention.
School Feeding Programs

These programs boost attendance AND nutrition simultaneously. For many children in low-income countries, school meals are their primary source of nutrition.
Girls' Education

Investing in girls' education has the highest ROI in development. Each additional year of schooling for girls reduces child mortality, increases future earnings, and reduces fertility rates.
EdTech for Remote Populations

Technology can extend educational access to underserved and remote populations — but only when paired with strong teacher support, not as a replacement for teachers.
Low-Cost Schools & Vouchers

Controversial and context-dependent, but in some settings, low-cost private schools and voucher programs have expanded access where public systems have failed.
How to Actually Do It
  1. Fund Room to Read and similar literacy organizations
  2. Advocate for girls' education in international aid budgets
  3. Support school feeding programs through WFP
  4. Push for teacher training investment in national education budgets
  5. Support community-based education programs in conflict zones
 What Digital Makers Can Do

You design and build digital things. Here's how those skills apply.

 Individual

Share what you know. Write tutorials, record screencasts, mentor someone earlier in their career. Make your educational content accessible — captions, alt text, plain language. The best thing a skilled maker can do is make more skilled makers.

 Community

Volunteer at local code clubs, libraries, and schools. Build free learning tools for community education programs. Help under-resourced schools set up and maintain their technology infrastructure.

 Country

Contribute to open-source educational platforms and open educational resources (OER). Build tools that help teachers in under-resourced schools create and share curricula. Design assessment tools that measure learning, not compliance.

 World

Build offline-capable educational tools for areas without reliable internet. Design multilingual learning interfaces that respect local pedagogical traditions. Contribute to platforms like Khan Academy, Wikipedia, and other free knowledge infrastructure that serves billions.

 Further Reading Teach For All

Global network developing collective leadership for education equity.

 Room to Read

Transforming the lives of millions of children through literacy and gender equality in education.

 CAMFED

Campaign for Female Education — tackling poverty and inequality through girls' education.

 Khan Academy

Free, world-class education for anyone, anywhere.

Built with the Eddie Design System by Brad Frost.
